verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes the inducible cytosolic Hsp70 chaperone, canonically intracellular. However, extensive literature documents stress/heat-shock-induced translocation of Hsp70 to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane of tumor and stressed cells, where it is detectable by flow cytometry on live non-permeabilized cells and by surface biotinylation. This tumor-selective surface display has been exploited therapeutically: NK-cell-based immunotherapies, antibodies (e.g., cmHsp70.1), and Hsp70-targeted drug strategies engage cell-surface Hsp70 on cancer cells. This is a well-documented oncogenic/stress-state ecto-pool for this specific protein, qualifying it as cell_state_induced. The mechanism of membrane association is debated (lipid-raft binding via the EEVD motif and charged regions; no classical TM domain), but surface residence is wash-resistant enough for antibody and NK-cell engagement in intact-cell assays. Baseline (non-stressed, non-transformed) cells do not display Hsp70 on the outer leaflet, confirming this is contextual rather than constitutive.
